title: PHP Source Graph Mapping (Adjacency Reference)
description: Single adjacency reference for how the PHP analyzer's facts are mapped into the Neo4j graph. The `legend` documents every node kind, label, property, relationship type, and edge property. The `graph` is a worked example: each node is keyed by its id and lists its outbound relationships (`out`), so the whole PHP source model can be read as one nested adjacency structure. Targets in `out[].to` reference other node ids in `graph`. See docs/architecture_world_mapping.md for the prose explanation.

legend.idScheme: A symbol id is its fully-qualified name (FQN) verbatim. A method id is `<owner FQN>::<method name>`. The id never encodes the kind; kind lives in the label and the `kind` property. PHP guarantees one symbol per FQN, so the FQN alone is a stable, kind-agnostic key.
legend.definedFlag: Every symbol carries `defined`. `true` when the analyzed file declares the symbol; `false` for referenced-only anchors (a parent class, an implemented interface, or a parameter/return type whose declaration is outside the analyzed scope). Referenced-only anchors that are never declared keep the bare `:Symbol:PHP` label and only an `fqcn` property until (if ever) their declaring file is indexed.

properties: returnType (scalar/fundamental spelling only; class types are RETURNS_TYPE edges)
paramTypes (scalar/fundamental spelling per param, '' when the param's type is purely class-like; aligned by index with paramNames)
parametersJson (per-parameter optional/variadic/byRef/promoted, stored as a JSON string because Neo4j cannot hold a list of objects)

PARAM_TYPE.to: class | interface | enum | trait (or kind-unknown anchor)
edgeProperties: identity (hash of from:TYPE:to:position, so two params of the same type stay distinct)
is_array (true only on docblock edges whose class is an array/list/iterable element; absent otherwise)

RETURNS_TYPE.to: class | interface | enum | trait (or kind-unknown anchor)
edgeProperties: is_array (true only on docblock array/list/iterable element edges)

DECLARED_IN_PACKAGE.from: class | interface | trait | enum (declared symbols only; methods inherit their owner's package)
DECLARED_IN_PACKAGE.to: Package (a node from the composer-lock graph)
DECLARED_IN_PACKAGE.note: Built by the separate index-links pipeline, not by source ingestion. Resolved in Cypher by the longest-matching PSR-4 namespace prefix of the symbol's FQN against Package.psr4Namespaces. Symbols loaded via classmap/psr-0 (e.g. Magento\Setup, legacy Zend, test classes) are not linked. Symbols under a namespace declared by two packages get one edge to each.

typeMappingRules.classLikeTypes: class/interface/enum/trait types become PARAM_TYPE / RETURNS_TYPE edges to the type's node.
typeMappingRules.scalarTypes: fundamental scalars (int, string, bool, float, array, void, null, ...) stay as the `returnType` / `paramTypes` string fields; they never become nodes (avoids scalar supernodes).
typeMappingRules.nativeVsDocblock: Native signature types are edges with source=native. Docblock @param/@return class types are added as edges with source=docblock ONLY when the class is not already present in the native set at that position (identity-dedup makes 'same type -> not added twice' automatic).
typeMappingRules.arrays: A docblock array element (Foo[], array<int,Foo>, list<Foo>, iterable<Foo>) becomes an edge to the element class with is_array=true; native `array` stays a scalar field.
typeMappingRules.scalarBackfill: When a parameter or return has no native type but the docblock supplies a fundamental scalar (e.g. an untyped param documented as `int`), that scalar backfills `paramTypes` / `returnType`.
typeMappingRules.dropped: Unresolvable, pseudo, or malformed docblock types (class-string, non-empty-string, names containing '-', unparsable text) are dropped entirely: no edge, no field.

package:magento/module-catalog.comment: A composer-lock graph node, linked in by the index-links pipeline via PSR-4 (Magento\Catalog\ prefixes the class FQN). Properties abbreviated here; see the composer-lock graph for the full Package shape.

Magento\\Catalog\\Model\\ProductReposito.comment: Constructor-promoted dependency: the DI graph is captured here as a PARAM_TYPE edge (no separate property node).
Native object param + scalar param (scalar stays a field, no edge) + docblock-only array-element param + native and docblock return.
Untyped param `id` backfills its scalar from the docblock; return type is documented only (no native return) so it is a docblock RETURNS_TYPE edge.

Magento\\Catalog\\Api\\ProductRepository.comment: Interface method: abstract with no body. Implementations are resolvable at query time via IMPLEMENTS/EXTENDS/USES + HAS_METHOD name match (no override edge stored).

Magento\\Catalog\\Model\\Product\\Visibi.comment: Enum: implements an interface and may use a trait, exactly like a class. Enum cases are not modeled as nodes.

Magento\\Framework\\Mview\\View::__const.comment: Union-typed parameter: `ConfigInterface|ConfigReader $config` fans out into two PARAM_TYPE edges that share name and position but target different nodes (distinct identities). A second param of the SAME type as another would also be its own edge, distinguished by position.

Magento\\Catalog\\Model\\AbstractReposit.comment: Referenced-only parent declared elsewhere in scope: emitted as a defined:false anchor so EXTENDS has an endpoint; gains its full properties when its own file is indexed.

Magento\\Catalog\\Api\\Data\\ProductTier.comment: Kind-unknown anchor: this type is only ever referenced (here as a docblock array element). Its declaring file was not in the analyzed scope, so the kind is unknown and the bare :Symbol:PHP label is used until (if ever) it is declared.
